title.en: FT, SA Query, TDLS, Self-Protected & Mesh Action Frames: roaming, anti-spoof, peer & mesh control
title.zh: FT、SA Query、TDLS、自保护与 Mesh 动作帧：漫游、防伪、对等与网格控制

blocks.en: Most 802.11 Action frames manage the plain STA-to-AP link. This lesson tours five categories that manage richer *relationships* — fast hand-off to a new AP, proof that you are really still there, a direct side-channel to another station, and the peerings and forwarding paths of a mesh. Each category owns one kind of relationship, and its Action frames are just that relationship's lifecycle messages: open it, confirm it, run it, tear it down. Read this as a guided tour, not a field dump — for each frame we name the one job it does and when it shows up.
blocks.zh: 大多数 802.11 动作帧（Action frame）管理的是普通的 STA-AP 链路。本课带你巡览五类管理更复杂“关系”的动作帧——快速切换到新 AP、证明你确实还在线、与另一台站点建立直连旁路，以及网格（mesh）中的对等关系与转发路径。每一类各负责一种关系，而它的动作帧不过是这种关系的生命周期消息：建立、确认、运行、拆除。请把它当作一次导览，而不是字段清单——对每一帧，我们只点出它做的那一件事，以及它何时出现。

blocks.en: Fast BSS Transition (FT, 9.6.8) is roaming without paying the full security tax. Normally hopping to a new AP means a fresh 4-way handshake; FT lets a station re-key at the target AP through its current AP, so the hand-off is fast. The over-the-DS exchange uses four frames carried as a transport between the STA and its current AP: the FT Request (9.6.8.2) the station sends to start the transition to a named target AP; the FT Response (9.6.8.3) the current AP returns with a status code; the FT Confirm (9.6.8.4) where the station confirms receipt of the AP's nonce and proves the key is live; and the FT Ack (9.6.8.5) the AP returns to close the round, optionally carrying the resource (RIC) response and a reassociation deadline.
blocks.zh: 快速 BSS 切换（FT，9.6.8）是一种“免去全套安全开销”的漫游。通常跳到新 AP 意味着重新做一次完整的四次握手；FT 则允许站点经由当前 AP、在目标 AP 上重新生成密钥，从而让切换变快。这套“经由 DS”的交换用四帧完成，它们作为站点与当前 AP 之间的传输载体：站点发出 FT Request（9.6.8.2）以发起向指定目标 AP 的切换；当前 AP 回以携带状态码的 FT Response（9.6.8.3）；站点用 FT Confirm（9.6.8.4）确认收到 AP 的 nonce 并证明密钥仍然有效；AP 再以 FT Ack（9.6.8.5）收尾，其中可选地携带资源（RIC）响应与重关联截止时间。

blocks.en: SA Query (9.6.9) is a tiny two-frame liveness check that defeats spoofed deauth/disassoc. If an attacker forges a disconnect, the AP doesn't just drop you — it sends an SA Query Request (9.6.9.2) carrying a Transaction Identifier, and a genuinely-present, genuinely-keyed station answers with an SA Query Response (9.6.9.3) echoing that same identifier. No valid response means the request was bogus; a valid one proves you are still there and keeps the association alive. The standard notes this is what prevents the Association Lockout problem.
blocks.zh: SA Query（9.6.9）是一次只有两帧的“存活性检查”，用来挫败伪造的去认证/解关联攻击。若攻击者伪造了断开消息，AP 不会直接把你踢掉——它会发出携带事务标识符（Transaction Identifier）的 SA Query Request（9.6.9.2），而一个真正在线、真正持有密钥的站点会回以 SA Query Response（9.6.9.3），并回显相同的标识符。收不到有效响应，说明那条断开请求是假的；收到有效响应，则证明你仍在线，从而保住关联。标准指出，这正是防止“关联锁定（Association Lockout）”问题的机制。

blocks.en:
sequenceDiagram
    participant STA as Station
    participant AP as Access Point
    Note over STA,AP: forged disassoc arrives at AP
    AP->>STA: SA Query Request (Transaction ID = t)
    STA->>AP: SA Query Response (Transaction ID = t)
    Note over STA,AP: STA proved liveness -> association kept
blocks.zh:
sequenceDiagram
    participant STA as 站点
    participant AP as 接入点
    Note over STA,AP: 伪造的解关联帧到达 AP
    AP->>STA: SA Query Request (事务ID = t)
    STA->>AP: SA Query Response (事务ID = t)
    Note over STA,AP: 站点证明存活 -> 保留关联

blocks.en: Notice the shared grammar across categories: a Request opens, a Response/Confirm settles terms, and a Teardown/Close ends it. FT Request -> Response -> Confirm -> Ack, TDLS Setup Request -> Response -> Confirm -> Teardown, Mesh Peering Open -> Confirm -> Close. Once you see the request/confirm/teardown rhythm, a new category is mostly just new payload elements riding the same skeleton.
blocks.zh: 注意各类之间共享的“语法”：一个 Request 打开，一个 Response/Confirm 敲定条款，一个 Teardown/Close 结束。FT Request -> Response -> Confirm -> Ack，TDLS Setup Request -> Response -> Confirm -> Teardown，Mesh Peering Open -> Confirm -> Close。一旦你看出这种“请求/确认/拆除”的节奏，面对新的一类时，往往只是同一副骨架上换了新的载荷元素而已。

blocks.en: TDLS (Tunneled Direct Link Setup, 9.6.12) builds a direct STA-to-STA link while both stations stay associated to the same AP. The setup frames are encapsulated in Data frames and tunneled through the AP: the TDLS Setup Request (9.6.12.2) proposes the direct link and advertises the initiator's capabilities; the TDLS Setup Response (9.6.12.3) carries the responder's status and capabilities; and the TDLS Setup Confirm (9.6.12.4) seals the negotiated parameters and the operating elements. When the side-link is no longer wanted, a TDLS Teardown (9.6.12.5) carries a reason code to dismantle it. (The same category also defines Channel Switch, Peer Traffic Indication/Response, and Peer PSM frames to steer and power-manage a live direct link — the same lifecycle idea applied to running, not just setting up.)
blocks.zh: TDLS（隧道直连建立，9.6.12）在两台站点仍关联到同一 AP 的同时，建立一条 STA-到-STA 的直连链路。建立类帧被封装进数据帧、经由 AP 隧道转发：TDLS Setup Request（9.6.12.2）提议建立直连并通告发起方能力；TDLS Setup Response（9.6.12.3）携带响应方的状态与能力；TDLS Setup Confirm（9.6.12.4）则敲定协商好的参数与运行元素。当不再需要这条旁路时，TDLS Teardown（9.6.12.5）携带原因码将其拆除。（同一类还定义了 Channel Switch、Peer Traffic Indication/Response 与 Peer PSM 等帧，用于对已建立的直连链路做信道切换与功耗管理——同样的生命周期思路，只是用在“运行”而非“建立”阶段。）

blocks.en: Self-Protected Action frames (9.6.15) manage mesh peerings, and they are called self-protected because, unlike robust Action frames, they cannot assume a security association already exists — they typically *create* one, carrying their own protection (the MIC and AMPE elements) when authenticated mesh peering exchange is enabled. The trio is Mesh Peering Open (9.6.15.2) to propose a peering, Mesh Peering Confirm (9.6.15.3) to confirm it, and Mesh Peering Close (9.6.15.4) to close it; the same category also defines Mesh Group Key Inform/Acknowledge to update the group key between peers. Once peered, the Mesh category proper takes over: the HWMP Mesh Path Selection frame (9.6.16.3) carries PREQ/PREP/PERR/RANN elements to establish, update, or delete forwarding paths across the mesh — and at the multihop layer, the Proxy Update frame (9.6.17.2) tells a peer about new, updated, or deleted proxy information for stations reachable beyond the mesh.
blocks.zh: 自保护动作帧（Self-Protected，9.6.15）管理网格对等关系；之所以叫“自保护”，是因为与健壮（robust）动作帧不同，它们不能假定安全关联已经存在——它们通常是去*创建*关联的，并在启用了“已认证网格对等交换（AMPE）”时自带保护（MIC 元素与 AMPE 元素）。这三件套是：Mesh Peering Open（9.6.15.2）提议对等、Mesh Peering Confirm（9.6.15.3）确认对等、Mesh Peering Close（9.6.15.4）关闭对等；同一类还定义了 Mesh Group Key Inform/Acknowledge，用于在对等节点间更新组密钥。一旦完成对等，Mesh 类本身接手：HWMP Mesh Path Selection 帧（9.6.16.3）携带 PREQ/PREP/PERR/RANN 元素，用于在网格中建立、更新或删除转发路径；而在多跳（Multihop）层，Proxy Update 帧（9.6.17.2）则把可经网格抵达的站点的代理信息——新增、更新或删除——告知对端。

blocks.en: These categories also differ in *how* they reach the peer. FT and SA Query go over the air to the AP; TDLS and Proxy Update ride inside Data frames tunneled through (or sent directly to) a peer; mesh path selection may be individually or group addressed depending on what it carries. The category isn't just a label — it tells you the delivery path and the relationship being managed.
blocks.zh: 这几类的差异还体现在它们*如何*抵达对端。FT 与 SA Query 通过空口发给 AP；TDLS 与 Proxy Update 则封装在数据帧里，经隧道（或直接）发给对端；网格路径选择则视所携带的内容，可能用单播或组播地址。类别不只是个标签——它告诉你投递路径，以及正在管理的是哪种关系。

summary.en: The Action-field tables that switch frame format within each category
summary.zh: 在每一类内部切换帧格式的“动作字段”取值表

blocks.en: Inside each category, the octet right after the Category field — the category's Action field — selects the exact frame format. The FT Action field (Table 9-510) maps 1=FT Request, 2=FT Response, 3=FT Confirm, 4=FT Ack; both FT Request and Response carry a STA Address and Target AP Address, and the Response/Ack add a 2-octet Status Code. The SA Query Action field (Table 9-515) maps 0=Request, 1=Response, each carrying a 2-octet Transaction Identifier and an optional OCI element. The TDLS Action field (Table 9-524) enumerates Setup Request/Response/Confirm, Teardown, Peer Traffic Indication, Channel Switch Request/Response, Peer PSM Request/Response, Peer Traffic Response, and Discovery Request; TDLS Setup frames are Data frames carrying the Action field tunneled through the AP (11.20.2). The Self-protected Action field (Table 9-546) maps 1=Mesh Peering Open, 2=Confirm, 3=Close, 4/5=Mesh Group Key Inform/Acknowledge. The Mesh Action field (Table 9-552) maps 1=HWMP Mesh Path Selection, whose frame (Table 9-554) carries optional PREQ/PREP/PERR/RANN elements. The Multihop Action field (Table 9-564) maps 0=Proxy Update, whose frame (Table 9-565) carries a Mesh Control field and one or more PXU elements.
blocks.zh: 在每一类内部，紧跟 Category 字段之后的那一个八位组——即该类的“动作字段”——决定具体的帧格式。FT 动作字段（表 9-510）：1=FT Request、2=FT Response、3=FT Confirm、4=FT Ack；FT Request 与 Response 都携带 STA Address 与 Target AP Address，而 Response/Ack 另加一个 2 字节的状态码。SA Query 动作字段（表 9-515）：0=Request、1=Response，二者都携带一个 2 字节的事务标识符与一个可选的 OCI 元素。TDLS 动作字段（表 9-524）列举了 Setup Request/Response/Confirm、Teardown、Peer Traffic Indication、Channel Switch Request/Response、Peer PSM Request/Response、Peer Traffic Response 与 Discovery Request；TDLS Setup 类帧是携带该动作字段、经由 AP 隧道转发的数据帧（11.20.2）。自保护动作字段（表 9-546）：1=Mesh Peering Open、2=Confirm、3=Close、4/5=Mesh Group Key Inform/Acknowledge。Mesh 动作字段（表 9-552）：1=HWMP Mesh Path Selection，其帧（表 9-554）携带可选的 PREQ/PREP/PERR/RANN 元素。Multihop 动作字段（表 9-564）：0=Proxy Update，其帧（表 9-565）携带一个 Mesh Control 字段与一个或多个 PXU 元素。

cites.note: 9.6.8.2-9.6.8.5 FT Request/Response/Confirm/Ack frames — over-the-DS fast BSS transition transport
9.6.9.2-9.6.9.3 SA Query Request/Response frames — Transaction Identifier liveness check; prevents Association Lockout
9.6.12.2-9.6.12.5 TDLS Setup Request/Response/Confirm and Teardown — Data frames tunneled through the AP (see 11.20)
9.6.15.2-9.6.15.4 Mesh Peering Open/Confirm/Close — self-protected mesh peering Management frames; AMPE protection
9.6.16.3 HWMP Mesh Path Selection (PREQ/PREP/PERR/RANN) and 9.6.17.2 Proxy Update (Mesh Control + PXU)
